subject: Sports
title: Athletes of Ancient Olympia
description: A fast breakdown of Athletes of Ancient Olympia for builders.

hook.text: Ancient Olympic champions were treated like gods—free meals for life, statues in their honor.

buildup.text: Milo of Croton won wrestling six times across 24 years. He supposedly trained by carrying a calf daily.

discovery.text: Leonidas of Rhodes won 12 individual crowns across four Games—a record that stood for 2,100 years.

twist.text: Some champions were caught cheating. Fines were used to build statues of Zeus along the entrance path.

climax.text: A victor's hometown might tear down city walls, believing a champion made fortifications unnecessary.

punchline.text: Ancient champions earned immortality, not medals.

quiz.question: How many wrestling titles did Milo of Croton win?
